query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed my research on PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan's ADIZ in May–June 2026. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Sustained 2026 decline:** PLA air activity has been at multi-year lows all year — each month January through May 2026 set the lowest sortie count for its respective month since the post-2022 baseline.

**The pause:** From **February 27 to March 5, 2026**, Taiwan's MND reported **zero PLA aircraft** near the ADIZ (~7-day pause), attributed mainly to domestic Chinese political-calendar events (NPC "Two Sessions," festivals).

**June 2026 figures:** 214 total aircraft sorties and 134 median-line crossings — both well below June 2024/2025. Notably, naval activity surged in the opposite direction: **225 PLAN vessels, a record June total**, suggesting a shift in emphasis from air to sea rather than overall de-escalation.

**Typical daily counts (recently):** Highly variable, roughly **1–22 aircraft/day**, averaging **~7/day in June 2026** — far below the 2024–2025 baseline of ~319 sorties/month (~10+/day). Recent examples: 9 (May 28), 2 (Jun 25–26), 1 (Jun 26–27), 22 (Jul 2), 8 (Jul 3–4).

**Highest single-day count in 2026:** Within 2026's day-to-day gray-zone activity, the peak was in the **low-to-mid 20s** (24 median-line crossings on Mar 18; ~29-sortie patrol days). The record-scale **130 aircraft / 90 median-line crossings** in one day belongs to the **Dec 29–30, 2025 "Justice Mission-2025" drill** (reported Jan 1, 2026) — technically a late-2025 event, and the second-highest one-day median-line total on record.

All findings, data points, and sources have been delivered via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang: Monthly Update on PLA Air and Maritime Maneuvers Around Taiwan (June 2026)
sources.snippet: In June 2026, the PLA conducted 134 median line crossings and 214 total aircraft sorties detected around Taiwan. Both figures remained substantially lower than 2024 and 2025. 225 PLAN vessels detected — highest June total on record.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: From February 27th to March 5th, 2026, Taiwan's MND reported no PLA aircraft operating near the ADIZ — an unusually long pause. Prior counts: 19 (Jan 29), 32 (Feb 12), 22 (Feb 26).

sources.title: Tracking China's Increased Military Activities in the Indo-Pacific in 2025 | ChinaPower Project (CSIS)
sources.snippet: PLA conducted 3,764 air incursions into Taiwan's ADIZ in 2025, up 22.4% from 2024. Post-May 2024 monthly average ~319 sorties; never below 209/month.

sources.title: Taiwan Security Monitor: 90 sorties crossed median line — second highest one-day total on record
sources.snippet: Per the MND, 90 sorties of PLA aircraft crossed the median line; second highest one-day total on record. 130 PLA aircraft, 14 PLAN vessels, 8 official ships detected (Justice Mission-2025, late Dec 2025).

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (Jul 3-4, 2026)
sources.snippet: 8 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 PLAN ships and 7 official ships detected; 6 of 8 sorties entered SW ADIZ.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (Jun 25-26, 2026)
sources.snippet: 2 sorties of PLA aircraft and 6 PLAN ships detected; both entered SW ADIZ.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 9 PLA aircraft sorties, 6 PLAN vessels around territory
sources.snippet: May 28, 2026: 9 sorties recorded, all 9 crossed median line into central and SW ADIZ.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 1 sortie of Chinese military aircraft, 7 vessels
sources.snippet: June 27, 2026: 1 sortie of PLA aircraft and 7 PLAN vessels detected.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 22 Chinese aircraft sorties (Jul 2, 2026)
sources.snippet: July 2, 2026: 22 sorties detected, 20 crossed the median line (including J-16, H-6, KJ-500).

sources.title: PLA sends 8 vessels, 24 aircraft across median line
sources.snippet: March 18, 2026: 24 military aircraft crossed the median line, entering northern, central, southwestern and eastern ADIZ.

sources.title: The PLA's Justice Mission-2025 Exercise Around Taiwan | Global Taiwan Institute
sources.snippet: Dec 29-30, 2025 large-scale exercise focused on sea and air combat readiness patrols around Taiwan.

key_facts: PLA aircraft sorties in each month from January through May 2026 were the lowest for their respective months since the post-2022 baseline (K. Tristan Tang / MND data).
June 2026: 214 total PLA aircraft sorties and 134 median-line crossings detected around Taiwan — both substantially below June 2024 and June 2025.
From February 27 to March 5, 2026, Taiwan's MND reported ZERO PLA aircraft near the ADIZ, an unusually long ~7-day pause attributed mainly to domestic Chinese political-calendar events.
Typical daily counts May–early July 2026 ranged from ~1–22 aircraft; June 2026 averaged roughly ~7 sorties/day (214 sorties over 30 days).
Highest single-day count clearly within 2026's gray-zone activity was in the low-to-mid 20s: March 18, 2026 had 24 aircraft crossing the median line; some patrol days reached ~29 sorties.

The record-scale single-day event was Dec 29–30, 2025 'Justice Mission-2025' drill (reported Jan 1, 2026): 130 PLA aircraft in one day, 90 crossing the median line — second-highest one-day median-line total on record.
Naval activity surged to a record: 225 PLAN vessels detected in June 2026, the highest June total on record, offsetting the decline in air activity.
Only one Joint Combat Readiness Patrol occurred in June 2026, fewer than prior years, though unusually prolonged.
Recent specific daily figures: May 28 = 9 sorties; Jun 25-26 = 2; Jun 26-27 = 1; Jul 2 = 22 (20 median-line crossings); Jul 3-4 = 8.
2025 baseline: 3,764 annual ADIZ air incursions (up 22.4% from 2024); post-May 2024 monthly average ~319 sorties, never below 209/month.
